volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 2.5.325 - Section 2.6.390)

sections.label: Section 2.5.325
sections.original: Nunc in fermento totast , ita turget mihi .
sections.translation: She ’ s now all in a ferment ; she ’ s swelling so against me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.326
sections.original: Ego edepol illam mediam diruptam velim .
sections.translation: By my troth , I could like her to burst in the middle .

sections.label: Section 2.5.327
sections.original: Credo edepol esse , siquidem tu frugi bonae es .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I fancy she will , if indeed you manage cleverly . But your amorousness , i ’ faith , is a cause of trouble to me ; your wife is at enmity with me , your son at enmity ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.330
sections.original: inimici familiares .
sections.translation: my fellow-servants at enmity .

sections.label: Section 2.5.330b
sections.original: Quid id refert tua ?
sections.translation: What matters that to you ? So long as ( pointing to himself ) this Jupiter only is propitious to you , do you take care and esteem the lesser Gods at a straw ’ s value .

sections.label: Section 2.5.333
sections.original: Nugae sunt istae magnae . quasi tu nescias ,
sections.translation: That ’ s great nonsense ; as if you didn ’ t know how suddenly your human Jupiters take to dying .

sections.label: Section 2.5.335
sections.original: responde : si tu Iuppiter sis mortuos ,
sections.translation: So after all , if you , my Jupiter , are dead and gone , when your realm devolves upon the lesser Gods , who shall then come to the rescue of my back , or head , or legs ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.338
sections.original: Opinione melius res tibi habeát tua ,
sections.translation: Affairs will go with you better than you expect , if I obtain this — the enjoyment of my Casina .

sections.label: Section 2.5.340
sections.original: Non hercle opinor posse , ita uxor acriter
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I do not think it possibly can be ; so earnestly is your wife striving that she shall not be given to me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.341b
sections.original: At ego sic agam :
sections.translation: But this way I ’ ll proceed : I ’ ll put the lots in an urn , and draw the lots for yourself and Chalinus . I find that the business has come to this pass ; it ’ s necessary to fight with swords hand to hand .

sections.label: Section 2.5.345
sections.original: Quid si sors aliter quam voles evenerit ?
sections.translation: What , if the lot should turn out different from what you wish ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.346
sections.original: Bene dice . dis sum fretus , deos sperabimus .
sections.translation: Speak with good omen . I rely upon the Gods ; we ’ ll trust in the Gods .

sections.label: Section 2.5.347
sections.original: Non ego istud verbum émpsim tittibilicio ;
sections.translation: That expression I wouldn ’ t purchase at a rotten thread , for all people are relying upon the Gods ; but still I ’ ve frequently seen many of those deceived who relied upon the Gods .

sections.label: Section 2.5.350
sections.original: St , táce parumper .
sections.translation: But hold your tongue a little while . ( Pointing .)

sections.label: Section 2.5.350b
sections.original: Quid vis ?
sections.translation: What is it you mean ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.350c
sections.original: Eccum exit foras
sections.translation: Why look ; here ’ s Chalinus coming from the house , out here , with the urn and the lots . Now , with standards closing , we shall fight .

sections.label: Section 2.6.353
sections.original: Fáce , Chaline , certiorem me , quid meus vir me velit .
sections.translation: Let me know , Chalinus , what my husband wants with me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.354
sections.original: Ille edepol videre ardentem te extra portam mortuam .
sections.translation: By my troth , he wants to see you burning outside of the Metian gate [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.6.355
sections.original: Credo ecastor velle .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I believe he does want that .

sections.label: Section 2.6.355b
sections.original: At pol ego hau credo , sed certo scio .
sections.translation: But , by my troth , I don ’ t believe it , but I know it for certain .

sections.label: Section 2.6.356
sections.original: Plus artificum est mihi quam rebar : hariolum hunc habeo domi .
sections.translation: I ’ ve got more men of business than I imagined : I ’ ve got this fellow , a Diviner , in my house . What , if we move our standards nearer , and go to meet them ? Follow me . ( Goes up to CLEOSTRATA and CHALINUS .) What are you about ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.358b
sections.original: Adsunt quae imperavisti omnia :
sections.translation: All the things are here which you ordered ; your wife , the lots , the urn , and myself .

sections.label: Section 2.6.359b
sections.original: Te uno adest plus quam ego volo .
sections.translation: By yourself only , there is more here than I want .

sections.label: Section 2.6.360
sections.original: Tibi quidem edepol ita videtur ; stimulus ego nunc sum tibi ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , so it seems to you indeed . I ’ m a stinger to you now ; I ’ m pricking that dear little heart of yours ; even now it ’ s palpitating from alarm .

sections.label: Section 2.6.362
sections.original: Tace , Chaline .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , Chalinus ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.362b
sections.original: Comprime istum .
sections.translation: Do make that fellow be quiet .

sections.label: Section 2.6.362c
sections.original: Immo istunc , qui didicit dare .
sections.translation: No , that fellow rather ( pointing to OLYMPIO ) , who has learned to misbehave [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.6.363
sections.original: Adpone hic sitellam , sortis cedo mihi . animum advortite .
sections.translation: Set the urn down here . ( CHALINUS puts it down .) Give me the lots : lend your attention now . But I did think , my wife , that I could have prevailed upon you thus far ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.365
sections.original: Casina ut uxor mihi daretur ; et nunc etiam censeo .
sections.translation: for Casina to be given me as my wife , and even now I think so .

sections.label: Section 2.6.366
sections.original: Tibi daretur illa ?
sections.translation: She , given to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.366b
sections.original: Mihi enim — ah , non id volui dicere :
sections.translation: Why yes , to me — dear me , I didn ’ t mean to say that . While I meant for myself [*] , I said him ; ( aside ) why really , while I ’ m wanting her for myself , I ’ ve already , i ’ faith , been chattering at random .

sections.label: Section 2.6.368b
sections.original: Pol tu quidem , atque etiam facis .
sections.translation: Upon my word , you really have ; and you are still doing so .

sections.label: Section 2.6.369
sections.original: Huic — immo hercle mihi — vah , tandem redii vix veram in viam .
sections.translation: For him — no , no ; for myself , i ’ faith [*] . Plague take it , at last , with great difficulty , I ’ ve got into the right road !

sections.label: Section 2.6.370
sections.original: Per pol saepe peccas .
sections.translation: Very often , i ’ faith , you are making your mistakes .

sections.label: Section 2.6.370b
sections.original: Ita fit , ubi quid tanto opere expetas .
sections.translation: Such is the case when you desire anything very much . But each of us , both he ( pointing to OLYMPIO ) and I , apply to you for our rights —

sections.label: Section 2.6.371b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: How ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.372
sections.original: Dicam enim , mea mulsa : de istac Casina huic nostro vilico
sections.translation: Why , I ’ ll tell you , my sweet . As to this Casina , you must make a present of her to this bailiff of ours .

sections.label: Section 2.6.373b
sections.original: At pol ego néque facio neque censeo .
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , I neither do make it , nor do I in tend it .

sections.label: Section 2.6.374
sections.original: Tum igitur ego sortis utrimque ⋯ iam .
sections.translation: In that case , then , I ’ ll divide the lots between them .

sections.label: Section 2.6.374b
sections.original: Quis votat ?
sections.translation: Who forbids you ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.375
sections.original: Optimum atque aequissimum istud esse iure iudico .
sections.translation: I judge with reason that that is the best and fairest way . In fine , if that happens which we desire , we shall be glad ; but if otherwise , we ’ ll bear it with equanimity . ( Giving a lot to OLYMPIO .) Take , this lot — take it ; see what ’ s written on it .

sections.label: Section 2.6.378b
sections.original: Vnum .
sections.translation: Number one .

sections.label: Section 2.6.378c
sections.original: Iniquom est , quia isti prius quam mihi dedit .
sections.translation: It isn ’ t fair , be cause that fellow has got one before me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.379
sections.original: Accipe hanc sis .
sections.translation: Take this , will you .

sections.label: Section 2.6.379b
sections.original: Cedo . mane , unum venit in mentem modo :
sections.translation: Give it me . Stop though ; one thing has just now come into my mind .

sections.label: Section 2.6.380
sections.original: vide ne quae illic insit alia sortis sub aqua .
sections.translation: Do you see that there ’ s no other lot in there by chance at the bottom of the water .

sections.label: Section 2.6.380b
sections.original: Verbero ,
sections.translation: Whip-rascal ! do you take me to be your own self ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.381b
sections.original: Nulla est . habe quietum animum modo .
sections.translation: There is none ; only set your feelings at rest .

sections.label: Section 2.6.382
sections.original: Quod bonum atque fortunatum sit mihi —
sections.translation: May it prove lucky and fortunate to me , a great mischance to you !

sections.label: Section 2.6.382b
sections.original: Magnum malum
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it will certainly fall to you , I fancy ; I know your pious ways . But stop a bit ; is that lot of yours of poplar or of fir ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.385
sections.original: Quid tu id curas ?
sections.translation: Why do you trouble yourself about that ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.385b
sections.original: Quia enim metuo , né in aqua summa natet .
sections.translation: Why , because I ’ m afraid that it may float on the surface of the water . ( They go up to the urn .)

sections.label: Section 2.6.386
sections.original: † Auge cave . conicite sortes nunciam ambo huc . eccere .
sections.translation: Capital !— take care ! Now then , both of you , throw your lots in here . ( Pointing to the urn .) Look now , wife , all ’ s fair . ( They throw them in .)

sections.label: Section 2.6.387b
sections.original: Noli uxori credere .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you trust your wife .

sections.label: Section 2.6.387c
sections.original: Habe animum bonum .
sections.translation: Be of good courage .

sections.label: Section 2.6.388
sections.original: Credo hercle , hodie devotabit sortis si attigerit .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I do believe that she ’ ll lay a spell upon the lots this very day , if she touches them .

sections.label: Section 2.6.388b
sections.original: Tace .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 2.6.389
sections.original: Taceo . deos quaeso —
sections.translation: I ’ ll hold my tongue . I pray the Gods —

sections.label: Section 2.6.389b
sections.original: Vt quidem tu hodie canem et furcam feras .
sections.translation: Aye , that this day you may have to endure the chain [*] and the bilboes [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.6.390
sections.original: Mihi ut sortito eveniat —
sections.translation: That the lot may fall to me .
